text.title: discriminated-union-output Decision Tree
text.summary: Decision routing: union vs flatten vs registry vs two-call.

decision-tree.root-question: Does the output space genuinely have 2 or more distinct shapes (different required fields per shape)?

branch.conclusion: Use a single model with all fields; no union machinery needed.

branch.question: Do the branches share more than ~90% of their fields?

branch.conclusion: Collapse to one model with a `kind` literal + branch-specific optional fields. Union machinery costs more than it saves.

branch.question: Is the set of branches open (user-defined / plug-ins)?

branch.conclusion: Use a registry pattern with free-string `kind` + runtime branch lookup; do NOT use a discriminated union.

branch.question: Does the decoder backend honour JSON Schema `discriminator` (OpenAI strict, Pydantic v2, Outlines, XGrammar)?

branch.conclusion: Use a discriminated union with `Annotated[..., Field(discriminator="kind")]`. Discriminator first in every branch.
Fall back to two sequential calls: (1) classify the kind with an enum, (2) extract the branch fields with the matching schema.

text.example: q-multiple-shapes → branch(true) → q-field-overlap → branch(low) → q-open-set → branch(closed) → q-decoder-supports → branch(true) → conclusion(r1-discriminator-first).
